Oracle Events
- Search for emails related to BrightLoop Analytics, Tessa Moreno, Jordan Whitfield, and Preethi Nair to find the BrightLoop relocation correspondence - Tessa's original request for Whitfield (Denver) and Nair (Raleigh), coordinator assignments (Suki Patel for Whitfield, Blessing Okafor for Nair), August 22 move date, and Mina's status update email confirming these details.
- Search for emails related to Canopy Health, Patricia Langford, Dr. Anand Mehta, and Fatimah Al-Rashidi to find the Canopy/Mehta executive relocation correspondence - Dr. Mehta's move from Boston to Chicago, equipment inventory (two Eizo RadiForce RX660 diagnostic monitors, SentrySafe filing cabinet requiring chain-of-custody documentation), Patricia's initial request with May 5 on-site target, and UrbanNest housing options (3 units presented).
- Search Slack for BrightLoop, Canopy, and Mehta discussions to find internal context on coordinator assignments, billing setup, and Fatimah Al-Rashidi's consolidated update, flagging two critical items - the housing selection deadline (EOD April 25) and the chain-of-custody documentation template as a blocker for the equipment packing workstream.
- Search Airtable Relocations table for Whitfield and Nair records to find recReloc00000000035 (Jordan Whitfield) and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair) - both with incorrect Move Start Date of 2026-04-21 and Move End Date of 2026-04-23 (both should be in August, move date is August 22), wrong Assigned Coordinator for Whitfield ("Javier Morales" instead of "Suki Patel"), and wrong Special Requirements for Nair ("Studio or 1BR, pet-friendly (has a cat)" instead of "2BR furnished, spouse joining, family-friendly area").
- Search the Airtable Relocations table for Dr. Mehta to confirm that no relocation record exists despite the move being fully tracked in Linear, CRM, QuickBooks, and email/Slack.
- Search the Airtable Client Accounts table for Canopy Health to find recAcct000000001 with Account Manager listed as "Catalina Dubois" instead of Mina Hashimoto. The agent must infer to check the Client Accounts table - not just the Relocations table - to discover this discrepancy.
- Search Linear for BrightLoop project issues to check whether tracking tickets exist for Whitfield and Nair. Find that no tickets exist for either relocation; only Jordan Ekwueme tickets (#322 Apartment Search, #323 Flight Booking, #324 Moving Coordination) exist in proj_brightloop.
- Search Linear for Canopy project issues related to Dr. Mehta to find 3 in-progress tickets all assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi - #343 Temporary Housing, #375 Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody, #396 Household Move.
- Search QuickBooks for BrightLoop invoices to find INV-2026-0305 ($10,000 for Whitfield/Nair standard relocation packages + equipment stipend management, due April 23, fully unpaid - 3 days overdue). Note: INV-2026-0308 ($11,350 for Simone Richter and Marcus Webb) is a separate invoice and should not be confused with the Whitfield/Nair invoice.
- Search QuickBooks for Canopy estimates and invoices to find EST-2026-0422 ($12,170 for Dr. Mehta's executive relocation, 7 line items).
- Search CRM for BrightLoop and Canopy deals to find Canopy deal crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb ($12,170, in-progress stage) and no active deal specific to the Whitfield/Nair relocations.
- Look up contact details for Tessa Moreno, Patricia Langford, David Chen, and Mina Hashimoto to obtain email addresses before sending check-in emails - tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io, david.chen@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com
- Update Jordan Whitfield's Airtable relocation record (recReloc00000000035) via airtable_update_records: Assigned Coordinator from "Javier Morales" to "Suki Patel", Move Start Date from 2026-04-21 to 2026-08-22, Move End Date from 2026-04-23 to 2026-08-24.
- Update Preethi Nair's Airtable relocation record (recReloc00000000036) via airtable_update_records: Special Requirements from "Studio or 1BR, pet-friendly (has a cat)" to "2BR furnished, family-friendly area (spouse joining)", Move Start Date from 2026-04-21 to 2026-08-22, Move End Date from 2026-04-23 to 2026-08-24.
- Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for Jordan Whitfield relocation tasks (apartment search, flight booking, moving coordination) assigned to Suki Patel (moveops_suki_patel) in proj_brightloop.
- Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for Preethi Nair relocation tasks (apartment search, flight booking, moving coordination) assigned to Blessing Okafor (moveops_blessing_okafor) in proj_brightloop.
- Create an Airtable relocation record (via airtable_create_record) for Dr. Anand Mehta in the Relocations table with Company: Canopy Health, Origin: Boston, Destination: Chicago, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Status: In Progress.
- Update the Canopy Health client account record (recAcct000000001) via airtable_update_records: Account Manager from "Catalina Dubois" to "Mina Hashimoto".
- Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com with a BrightLoop check-in covering Whitfield/Nair relocation status updates, Airtable corrections made (coordinator, dates, requirements), and a follow-up on invoice INV-2026-0305 ($10,000, due April 23, overdue) requesting payment status.
- Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io with a Canopy check-in covering Dr. Mehta relocation status, flagging that the housing selection deadline (EOD April 25) has passed without a confirmed selection from the 3 UrbanNest options, and requesting the chain-of-custody documentation template from Canopy's compliance team to unblock the equipment packing workstream before the April 30 target pack date.
- Send an email (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com with a comprehensive standup summary covering both accounts - BrightLoop: Whitfield/Nair Airtable corrections, missing Linear tickets created, INV-2026-0305 ($10,000) overdue since April 23; Canopy/Mehta: housing deadline passed (EOD April 25), chain-of-custody template still missing as blocker, no Airtable relocation record existed (now created), 3 Linear workstream tickets in progress with Fatimah.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

28 The model must search the Airtable Client Accounts table (e.g., pass a table_name related to "Client Accounts" or search/list records from a table containing account-level data such as "tblClientAccts01" or similar) when calling airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records, to look up account information for Canopy Health and/or BrightLoop Analytics.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent called airtable_list_tables and found the 'Client Accounts' table (tblClientAccts01), but never called airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on that table. All Airtable record queries were on 'Relocations' and 'Stipend Transactions' tables only.
✗Run #2While the agent called airtable_list_tables and saw the 'Client Accounts' table (tblClientAccts01) existed, it never called airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with table_name 'Client Accounts'. The agent only searched the 'Relocations' and 'Stipend Transactions' tables.
✗Run #3The agent only searched the 'Relocations' and 'Stipend Transactions' tables in Airtable. No search of a 'Client Accounts' table was performed, so the outdated Canopy Health account record with Catalina Dubois as AM was never discovered.
✗Run #4Although the airtable_list_tables call revealed the 'Client Accounts' table (tblClientAccts01), the agent never called airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with table_name 'Client Accounts'. All subsequent Airtable searches used only 'Relocations' and 'Stipend Transactions' tables.
✗Run #5The agent only queried the 'Relocations' table in Airtable (airtable_search_records calls all used table_name 'Relocations' or 'Stipend Transactions'). No search was performed on a 'Client Accounts' table, so the outdated Canopy AM record was never discovered.
✗Run #6All airtable_search_records calls used table_name 'Relocations' or 'Stipend Transactions'. No call used a Client Accounts table. The agent never searched the Client Accounts table to discover the Catalina Dubois / Mina Hashimoto discrepancy.
